sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see the white bars at top and bottom are growing. Node 1 is W at row 0, cols 50-63 (14 pixels), and Node 7 is W at row 63, cols 0-13 (14 pixels). Earlier they were 12 pixels each. So they grow by 2 pixels per action.
The squares are at [19, 19] and [44, 44]. Both on the diagonal.
Let me think about what the goal could be. Maybe the goal is to get the squares to specific positions where they "lock" into place. Or maybe the goal is to get both squares to the same row or column.
Actually, let me reconsider the movement mechanics:
- UP/DOWN: both squares move in the same direction vertically.
- LEFT/RIGHT: squares move in opposite directions horizontally.
So if I want to get both squares to the same row, I can use UP/DOWN. But they're currently at rows 19-23 and 44-48, which are 25 rows apart (5 steps).
If I want to get both squares to the same column, I can use LEFT/RIGHT. But they move in opposite directions, so they can never be at the same column (unless they start at the same column, which they don't).
Wait, actually, the squares move in opposite directions horizontally. So if the left square is at col 19 and the right square is at col 44, pressing RIGHT would move the left square to col 24 and the right square to col 39. Pressing RIGHT again would move the left square to col 29 and the right square to col 34. Pressing RIGHT again would move the left square to col 34 and the right square to col 29. So they would cross each other!
But wait, the left square is in the yellow area (cols 0-31) and the right square is in the orange area (cols 32-63). If the left square moves to col 34, it would be in the orange area. And if the right square moves to col 29, it would be in the yellow area. So they would swap sides!
Maybe the goal is to get the squares to swap sides? Or to get them to specific positions?
Let me try pressing RIGHT a few times to see what happens. But I need to be careful not to waste too many actions.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The left square is at [19, 19] and the right square is at [44, 44]. If I press RIGHT:
- Left square moves right: [19, 24]
- Right square moves left: [44, 39]
If I press RIGHT again:
- Left square moves right: [19, 29]
- Right square moves left: [44, 34]
If I press RIGHT again:
- Left square moves right: [19, 34]
- Right square moves left: [44, 29]
So after 3 RIGHT actions, the left square would be at [19, 34] (orange area) and the right square would be at [44, 29] (yellow area). They would have swapped sides!
But I'm not sure if that's the goal. Let me try pressing RIGHT once to see if the squares can move.
Actually, I realize I should focus on finding the goal. Let me look at the initial state again:
- Left square: [49, 14]
- Right square: [49, 44]
Both squares were at row 49. The left square was at col 14 (yellow area), and the right square was at col 44 (orange area).
The goal might be to get both squares to the diagonal (row == col). The left square is now at [19, 19] (on diagonal), and the right square is at [44, 44] (on diagonal). So both squares are on the diagonal.
But SPACE didn't complete the level. Maybe I need to do something else after getting both squares on the diagonal.
Let me try using MOUSE to click on one of the squares. Maybe clicking on a square "activates" it or completes the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the left square with MOUSE
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 21}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
